Accruals & prepayments could be easier. One of our biggest issues is lack of granularity for user permissions. This is still a bit too clunky. We would like a paid phone support option or a live chat support. Although support is prompt, it's still very stop/start by email

Reporting could be a little more graph based to make them more intuitive but apart from that, really good reports. It would also be good if you could further customise the dashboard and the cash in/out timeframe. Seeing this for the whole financial year would be good as well as having the ability to compare against your budget so you can see how you are performing against your forecasts.

The navigation sometimes takes a few more clicks. So if I want want to view all invoices it takes 3 clicks. Also the timeout is very quick. Sometimes it can be confusing as to how the "contacts" work as they aren't assigned a customer or vendor status until they are actually used and then they can flip back/worth. So that can make life a little bit confusing than before

I don't like that the system won't allow for overpayments or for open credits to sit on accounts. Further, I find that often times PO's remain opened even after a final bill has been created which occasionally cases confusion.
To be honest I haven't actually found something yet which I dislike , I have been a user now for 7 months and really love the software,
really steep learning curve takes a little while to grasp. Color schemes could use a little revamping as well as the interface as a whole

There is not much I dislike about Xero. If I had a chance to make one thing better I would say the ability for Xero to pull in my bank account balance but I do not believe our inability to do that is something caused by Xero, our bank is just not easy to work with and requires us to manually update Xero.

There are a few integrations that it could use that would make it perfect. But they are not mission critical (different payment processors for receiving payment on invoices, for example.) . Also, I'd expect a company like Xero to be adding or improving features more frequently than they do.

I have noticed that they tend to have poor customer service. They rarely reply to tickets and sometimes if they do reply it does not clear anything up. That being said, they have a great community which helps you find solutions. If it was a little cheaper, the package would be perfect.
There’s not a lot I don’t like. If I had to pick one thing it would be the Payroll feature. If it is used incorrectly it is extremely difficult to fix.
Setting this service up was a PAIN. I hate dealing with accounting anything, and honestly quickbooks was a way easier and painless setup. But now that I have it up and running it's going okay.

-Limited options/programming for intercompany transactions. - Bank reconciliation can be tricky. You need to have a certain format .cvs and have to copy paste the content to the new sheet before the software can take it.
It can sometimes be difficult to find help as previous problems with reconciling resulting in some time in solving the problem.
Nothing notable, the reporting drop downs can take a little white to get used to.
The fact that they discontinued their version for home use.
Xero imported my quickbooks data, but it took me two tries to massage the data just right... I wish they offered telephone support!
Sometimes a bit slow and doesn't connect well with WI-FI
Some tools can be very slow to load, especially Payroll. The design is clearly a work in progress as it is disjointed with old and new designs spread throughout the site.
Good, but not great with inventory, but has great apps to link with. Internal Payroll Processing needs to be better.
I haven't had this issue personally, but a handful of other bookkeepers I know say that they have poor customer service.
Limited functionality in budget manager.
Only difficulty we've had is with purchase order numbers - as far as I know these can only be added in the invoice description.

The software is anything but beautiful. Let’s be honest, Excel is prettier that their platform. Xero is boxy at best and doesn’t have the reporting functionality of something like Google Sheets. Best for small businesses.

Since I am not in the system all the time I find that sometimes I am not sure if an invoice is really sent to a client. I tend to make sure I am cc'd. We also have some issues with workflow where the approver may send a draft to a client because they can see an invoice created right away.
Support sometimes feels a little remote / too community based.
If the internet is slow, sometimes it's hard to open the website and it's hard to get to the website in some countries.
The mobile application can include more features, but since I'm a developer. I know that the mobile application space will eventually move back to the cloud anyway.

First and foremost, if you need to use Purchase Orders, Xero isn't for you. You cannot include Item Codes (their version of Part Numbers) on purchase orders. You can use the Item Name, but even to get that you need to create a custom template and rework it entirely in Word to get that. The default setting from Xero is that you only get Item Description. This is obviously useless for any purchasing, and Xero has clearly stated that it is never going to happen because, and I quote, "Item Codes are mainly for internal purposes." It's a huge issue. https://community.xero.com/business/discussion/3361072 is the thread on their boards where they refuse to fix it. In addition, accountants can't make certain kinds of journal entries which they're used to being able to do in Quickbooks. This causes an issue if you're making the switch, so make sure your bookkeeper or accountant is trained on Xero.
I really cannot fault this program - I have been using it for many years and it is just easy to use.
It's hard to combine vendors if you've accidentally spelled a vendor wrong.
Blue is not my favourite colour, but its okay.
There is nothing i dislike to Xero. It aall works how i want it to work and does what i need.
Payroll in Xero is not usable in specific states in the United States.
I don't care for the dashboard as much as I do Quickbooks Online's. The sync feature for our bank account is cumbersome and seems to take a long time.
Cannot automatically integrate with my bank so have to manually import bank statements
Some "shortcuts" provided in Xero can cause minor mistakes. Make sure to take your time when going through
Wish that there was more direct education for those who aren't finance majors or accountants trying to manage the software, had to work with an accountant to get this type of stuff settled.
I have yet to have any major problems with Xero
Issue with bank feeds, not sure if this is a Xero problem or bank issue.

I haven't found anything too significant that's negative in Xero, but some add-ons do cost an additional fee. Also, the fact that it is web-based is a huge benefit but can sometimes play against you. If you need to check something on account, you are required to be connected to the internet otherwise the software will not work.
There hasn't been much update on the system recently.
I found reporting to be a bit lackluster. I wish Xero also did a better job tracking different locations.
As a Canadian user, we do see some limitations how xero functions. For example, we cannot create a CSV direct from xero to upload to our bank to create an EFT/ACH.

The customer service is customer dis-service. They lie and send erroneous information. They also have poor internal controls so when there is a system malfunction, they want to blame it on the customer. Worst of all when you take them up on their short-comings, they go into overdrive and in my case, they even tried to run my credit without my EXPLICIT consent. Even Banks would ask you nicely!
It can be annoying to add users and manage their access
I don’t know if I’ve found anything i don’t like. I’ve used other software and it just isn’t as easy as xero.
Having to remove & redo reconciled payments before being able to edit the invoices. Not easy to quickly pull sales data by customer for sales reporting. Difficult to manage contacts.

As an online software company, it is usual to have bugs when a new version is released. However, when Xero has bugs it changes things that can cause issues in your accounting system i.e. changing a customers invoice to GST INCL instead of EXCL etc. If you make changes to a repeating invoice, journal etc the notes give a time and a date but not what was changed. Help is only available through email which is fine most of the time but sometimes you do need someone to speak to.
I’d like for Xero to incorporate some more visual reports and graphs with regards to cash flow - much like Quickbooks.
Starting to get a bit over complex now as there is so much getting added to the product, I know many people that are turned off by this although I am used to it.
I dislike the fact you cannot separate invoice balance dues. Meaning a first half deposit and final balance due invoice.
Limited features in other country versions.
The app on my iPad has been a little buggy since I updated IOS.
Very financial/accountant based. Need financial understanding or background to use.
It can be challenging to customize reports without going through the tutorials.
Some of the reports are a little wonky, I have to export data to make a few reports in Excel.
I wouldn't say it is the best software we could be using. There are some things that make it confusing.
Does do consolidation or more complex multicurrency functionality particularly well

I find it difficult to get reports to show in the formats I want. Xero is from New Zealand, there are pieces of information in the software that are irrelevant to US taxpayers which can be cumbersome.
The phone app is not as expansive or fully functional as I would like. It can be used but not the best. It is sometimes easier to log in through my phones web browser than through the app.
Prices might be a bit high for a very small businesses owners
I don’t like that the mobile app doesn’t allow you to view existing invoices and it doesn’t allow you to run reporting functions.
I have not tried contacting their support yet, that means no issues so far using Xero :)
Reporting not entirely straightforward and searching for specific items not intuitive
price is a little too high for very small businesses.
The user interface is "big" and wasteful of space.
It would be great if it handled SAAS software subscriptions, and had more HR functions covered in the payroll solution
The biggest challenge for me was the conversion from QuickBooks to Xero if the accounts payable and accounts receivable were not cleaned up prior to conversion.

Xero is still in development and missing some features, and the product support team is very inefficient at answering queries. There is no option to automatically send invoices out at a certain time. Payroll is slightly clunky.
There could be more standard reports available
Doesn't allow multiple companies under a single account.
Most support is handled through external advisors, rather than from the Xero team themselves.
Not much; Reports can be a bit difficult to properly setup.
It lacks class categorizations to do job cost accounting
The general ledger report is not very easy to use. Xero should design the GL report like the QB GL report
Really nothing I can think of, every feature I have felt was missing since I started using it last year has been added since!
A few of their APIs have restrictions (especially around inventory)
We have been awaiting payroll in our state since signing up, we are on to year 2 with Xero. Every time we ask it's either coming by then end of the year or we're told it's been pushed back.

My dislikes are more along the lines of needed enhancements. Cash coding allows for bulk classification of transactions, but needs some work to streamline its interface with contacts. Bank feeds are facilitated by Yodlee which frequently requires refreshing to keep transactions up to date. Both are minor annoyances that are far outweighed by the efficiencies that Xero brings to the accountant. I would also love to see an interface with popular tax prep products such as Drake or ATX.

When you are drilling down into the expenditure type it's difficult to get back to the overview report. you need to use the browser back button that usually it does not work at all and it is a bad experience for the user.

I cannot think of anything that doesn't work how I would like it to in the main accounting system. The payroll system doesn't seem to be able to automatically update with changes to staff's tax codes, which seems like quite a basic requirement.
Sales tax treatment in Canada is not very elegant.
The api for xero can sometimes be difficult but it’s mostly ok

Support is only available via email, which makes it harder to explain and for them to understand some issues. Sometimes a phone call is faster then 10 emails. Each company has its own peculiarities and explaining them by email makes it hard.
I can't set up bank rules for transfers between my accounts, and I can't import XML documents (not that I'm aware of anyway)
1099 process at year end needs to be streamlined and created.
Cloud synchronization can be slow at times. Terminology is clearly designed for another market. It's Payroll in North America, not Pay Run. Not a huge issue though.
Easy to make a mistake in not submitting your expenses and you aren't notified if you have not yet done that.
I feel the reporting could be more robust, but at the same time, I have had no major issues using their tech to date.

Expense management, especially for employees is terrible. Their response is to suggest you subscribe to another company's expense software or do weird workarounds via invoicing. For a start-up with employees seeking reimbursement of out of pocket expenses, it is a real negative experience.

Website app is not as user friendly as it could be, mobile app is much easier to use. Also, difficult to use behind a firewall like Samsung Knox, but this is not a problem unique to Xero. Reporting functionality connected to the dashboard for the company could be better, seems like only certain fields are able to be pulled through API connection
Some of the more intricate reporting commonly done in QB can be a little tricky to recreate exactly.
Can't really think of that much negatives, even though I could imagine in larger organisations there will be certain features that could be improved. For a small business it's great as is!
There's not much I dislike. Other than the monthly fee :-) I guess the reports are a little tricky, but that's coming from someone with zero accounting experience.
It would be great if they could provide a more tight filter for invoices.

There are features which are hidden (e.g. Trust/Fund reporting) from people who are not tax agents or registered accountants. The support is only delayed/online - you cannot call, chat or email a real person! There are some irate people in the Xero Community discussion forums and some who have had to move to other products because it was too frustrating for them (I was ALMOST one of those myself, if it had not been for my superb financial controller who got us out of a jam). The multi-currency FX feature exists but NOT for expense claims (the most common need my business has for FX)!
The invoicing templates could use more flexibility so that I could potentially create a fully customized invoice that would replicate what I do in Google Slides currently.
absolutely nothing, Xero nails our company finances!
Cost wise its quite pricey compared to some of the competjtion out there.
Payroll has been a slightly challenging but we got there.

Some of the Dashboard elements would be better if you could combine or collapse them (e.g. Bank Accounts - you might just want the overall cash position, not a list of all your accounts). But that aside this is minor and the platform is a great tool.
We dislike that they didn't do more to help us migrate/transition from Quickbooks to Xero. The extent of their migration assistance was sending us links to information.
There's a definite learning curve, getting it configured and getting the team up to speed
I would really like to be able to pay an expense through the bill.com integration, but only invoices are synced. The mobile app should offer the ability to view outstanding quotes.
No live bank feeds with dbs business banking.

The product is incomplete. it is missing a lot of features that the community have been asking for for over 3-4 years and they have not made any progress. No changes, updates or improvements except at the high end obviously for their favouritre "large" customers. Then they implement price increases on the lowend users who've received no improvements at all

There choice of vendors, specifically Stripe credit card processing, ends up being a poor reflection for Xero. Stripe has over $16,000 of our money and we have received $0. XERO will not help in contacting Stripe. When I contacted XERO support, they said I had to speak to Stripe. I told them I had tried 5 times with no response, they said XERO couldn't help.
Nothing, the software is easy to use and available on all devices.
For the amount of use that I have, and the type of use, I can't fault anything.
Although I liked the reconciliation function, I wish you were able to input the data directly from your bank account by syncing it instead of having it upload and you having to review it.
You will need to run training to get the most from Xero. Occasional bugs.

Lack of useful functionality, lack of responsive support. The Xero team seem more interested in gaining new customers than listening to existing/paying customers, or implementing effective tools to make a better product.
Not all the features it apps available to QBO are available on xero
I do not dislike anything about Xero. Much better than my former "solution", Quickbooks

The payroll aspect is not fully developed and can not compete with other dedicated payroll processors on price. Once payroll gets complete across all 50 states and offers full paperless capability, Xero will completely outclass its rivals.

Processes are slow, slow slow! Direct Deposit takes 3-5 days for Xero to confirm, then another 5-7 days for their direct deposit partner to confirm. At each stage you think you are done until you try to process payroll. The time it takes and the processes related to setting up electronic payments are unacceptable. The software is not set up to be compatible with some common web browsers Xero says they will respond within 24 hours to questions but there is no assistance on weekends - when do they think small businesses do their accounting? Overall I still like the package but believe they are deleting negative reviews. The reviews I've read imply the software is outstanding, which it is not.
There are some features of Xero that could be improved, including accessibilty to client payment history
I would like more report filtering options.
At first, it can be intimidating to use, but with the help of support, I was able to figure it all out!

Unfortunately, after using Xero for two months the bank feed stopped working. After going back and forth with Zero support for about a month they finally called me and told me that my problem was a known one that affects a handful of Xero users and at this time they have no workable solution for my account except recommend I manually import my bank transactions indefinitely, because they pretty much had no solution to my problem. I was told it could take days, or months or even never to resolve the problem. Had I known this would happen I would have gone with Quickbooks. When I asked if I could receive a discount on my service since it was handicapped they offered 30 days free for the month it took them to tell me they couldn't fix my problem but otherwise could not offer any further discounts.

It is not necessarily intuitive, and you will need training from someone who knows what they are doing and I highly recommend working with an accountant who is familiar with it so that they can set it up and test it for you.
1. Reporting module needs a bit improvement. More robust reporting templates are needed.

Unfortunately Xero is for only sole traders or people who do small / tiny business and don't care about growth. Xero support is beyond bad, there's no phone support and emails are usually answered after a day or two. In case where the business is literally running on its toes, growing quickly and need to adapt very quickly to any change, lack of support is a deal breaker. There's also a few things that are really confusing (like attaching a file to the invoice, you need to attach it a couple of times to make it work) but according to Xero they're working on it.
I don't really have any complaints in this department. My accountant seemed to think it wasn't as robust as QB but was definitely workable.
I basically like everything, so far no complain.
That I have to remember to go in monthly and reconcile my accounts. But that's my issue :-)

As highlighted by another use, journals aren't particularly well provided for. In addition, wages in the UK can't be used to submit anything to HMRC as they require & finally the integrations are often a one way affair which means that they aren't perfect, Xero needs to work more closely at working back with other providers.
Lack of inventory/job costing interactivity.

Xero focuses on automation. It works best if you link it directly with bank accounts, credit cards, etc. But many clients are wary of that kind of access and prefer to provide me with Excel spreadsheets. Xero requires multiple steps in order to convert an Excel spreadsheet into data that Xero can understand and import. I think that integration with Excel is a pretty basic requirement for accounting software and Xero hasn't provided it. Further more, manual entries are cumbersome. Xero forces you to do them one at a time, instead of providing a general ledger type of entry. I realize that these complaints are a little "old school" and Xero is focused more on moving away from out of date bookkeeping methods - but not all of my clients are ready for new technology. As a result, I tend not to recommend Xero to anyone unless they are ready for complete automation. Xero doesn't seem to make it easy for clients to grant direct access to their accountants. Most of my clients provide me with their actual login credentials. Actually, it does seem to be easy, just not obvious - Xero should work harder to make clients AND accountants aware of this feature. Xero requires that accountants endorse/sell Xero on their website in order to get free access. Quickbooks Online doesn't require this, and I think it discourages accountants from trying Xero.
Nothing - everyone in the company is extremely responsive, there is xero (pun intended) down time, and the bank feeds are an amazing time saver on reconciliation.
The overall format is a little annoying.

It describes itself as multicurrency, but it's not, and it's clear that the design of the thing is just flawed. There's a feature request from 2012 about making personal expenses work if you have expenses in another currency. You know, one of the basic multicurrency functions. It doesn't work, and the company line seems to be that it's a complicated feature. From a developer's point of view you'd expect that there'd be a module that would say something like "input money", and that they should be able to point the expenses function there, but no. Apparently it's too complicated. And this is a product they market in the EU. The whole multicurrency thing kind of doesn't work terribly well. It's as though someone added it without thinking about what it would be like for the user.
Converting data from QuickBooks or QB online is still not great. Though there is a conversion tool, it still requires more manual work than most would guess.

There are limits around API access, which can be frustrating. Also, the limit of two tracking categories is often too restrictive for the elements we'd like to track. The reporting is also somewhat limited.

As has been mentioned by other users, the journal updating process can be a bit tedious. However, my experience has been that Xero has been willing to listen to their users and I suspect this will be addressed. The reporting leaves room for improvement, but like I stated above, there are numerous tools available in the marketplace that offer great options.

Some functionality beyond the basic is a little convoluted to get to, and whilst some reports are set up in a handy style for the technicalities of accounting, they are slightly too complex and technical for a causal user who just wants to see a bit of BI.
Very simple functionality, but that's expected. Will need to upgrade to an ERP.

The reporting could be improved, which I know they are working on. Ability to customize is limited. The cash flow statement is terrible. For one company we go through a lot of extra work to prepare financials just because we need to prepare a better cash flow statement in Excel. The Global version doesn't have check printing capability, which is a large drawback. You can't batch pay invoices that aren't in the functional currency. It isn't a huge deal, but it takes extra work to pay individual bills and to reconcile separate payments in Xero with one payment in the bank account.
I honestly haven't yet found an issue. That said, I don't currently use the Payroll component nor need to worry about currency conversion.
Nothing comes to mind here... it's honestly incredibly easy to use and way more flexible than the alternatives on the market.
UK payroll still a little under baked. Feels like they rushed it through a bit.
I am disappointed to find they are not handling multiple currencies very well. For me that is a vital feature for my business.
The Dislike is a bit more difficult to nail down. In the beginning of the product it was the fact the Payroll was not included. Now they have that so it is a full package.

There is a fairly manual process of updating journals, which I use regularly so this is one area they could improve. However it would be safe to assume the majority of Xero end users would not need to use journals. This is because journals are normally done by accounting professionals rather than small business owners.
Wish our "small town bank" played nicer.

P.O. functionality and reporting could be a bit better, but they're working on it. If you aren't familiar with pivot tables, it can be a bit more effort to get the information you need. However, if you are familiar with pivot tables the world is your oyster. It's not necessarily for everyone. Some people can't think differently from Quickbooks. And that's okay. But, those who are more tech-oriented and grew up with all things internet will feel more at home here.
Allocations of credits is cumbersome Audit trail is not as good as other packages

Currently Xero does not offer payroll for all U.S. states and the financial reporting functions leave a bit to be desired. A quick search of the add-on partners may provide some small businesses with additional reporting capabilities. Still waiting on U.S. tax software integration also.

The VAT return is quite inflexible in terms of what you can include and exclude from the return. There needs to be more options around making manual adjustments to the return. It would also make the product more useable if it could handle partial exemption companies. An additional option to produce statutory accounts in IXBRL format would also be very useful. The link to the bank account does not always work well. There are often transactions that are missed off. Fixing this bug would make the software much better.
Expensive to adopt if just starting out. May not make since for sole practitioner or client with fewer than 5 employees.
If there is anything that Xero is lacking it can be found in the add-on community that plugs directly into Xero

SAAS model makes revenue recognition painful - we accept $ up front, but recognize it over 12 months. This is a fairly common model in SAAS, but is painful to configure in Xero - i.e. requires splitting invoices, etc. This needs fixing. Reporting is more painful than it needs to be - use Spotlight (Xero should just buy them). Expense reporting needs an offline app for iOS/Android.

Not having enough time to actually use the software! They make it pretty easy to manage finances that you're in and out so quickly. The interface is a little spartan and dated, and I wish you could do a tad more visual branding in the dashboard so the internal staff are working with my brand represented better, but it's workable.
There isn't anything I dislike about Xero. Any special features that I require are available via SaaS integration and will likely become a native feature as people show an interest.

I do not like the fact that you can not call into a technical support person while you are in the middle of an issue. I am just learning Xero and do have questions from time to time. I can get answers to my questions, but it would be an improvement if I could talk to someone live while I am working on my issue.

You still need a lot of technical accounting knowledge to use their system. In an ideal world, Xero would let any user manage their finances solo but the reality is that we had to work with a CPA in order to get everything set up and squared away perfectly.
I love to see a forecasting tool built into the tool.
Payroll has some issues regarding assigning checks. No support for local submittal of tax forms
Not much to dislike really it's easy to use and provides me with great reporting on where my business is at financially very quickly and easily.
My bank feeds don't always work, however, they are often remedied by my accountant quickly.
Have not found anything I dislike. I would like for the US Based Payroll for all States to be available sooner
Nothing at all. Seriously. I find it meets our needs totally.
Reporting is nowhere near where it needs to be for either a full scale accounting service or a client with complex reporting requirements
- Unavailability of live support - Limited invoice format
Could use better Salesforce.com integration

For a company that "gets" accounting so well I am staggered that the mobile app only shows money coming in, not that going out. How am I supposed to get any idea of cashflow without it? To be honest it renders the app pretty superfluous. German tax accounting is too complicated for Xero so we can't use it for our German company.
I don't like that you cant integrate many apps like in quickbooks. I think quickbooks online is a better solution personally.

Xero's biggest issue is that is doesn't yet automatically tie in with all banks so we currently have to upload our bank statements on to an excel and then upload them to xero, but once they are up there it's fine, you just need to make sure your not uploading any duplicate lines on your statement.
Sometimes it is difficult to navigate in Xero but after a few moment of jogging my memory I can usually find what I need.
Lack of support for multi-currency expense reports. (Xero handles multi-currency invoices and other things, but not expense reports).
